reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All six gates passed. Gate 1: verify_data_column_sidecar is actively used in gossip validation path (data_column_verification.rs:225, 480; network_beacon_processor/gossip_methods.rs:634). Gate 2: Attack via P2P gossip (untrusted). Gate 3: Code reading verified—block_verification.rs:883-886 enforces max_blobs_per_block(epoch) runtime check (e.g., 6 for Electra), but data_column_verification.rs:532-561 omits this check entirely, only verifying array length consistency. SSZ VariableList accepts up to MaxBlobCommitmentsPerBlock=4096. Gate 4: An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by sending a data column sidecar with kzg_commitments.len() > max_blobs_per_block(epoch), causing resource exhaustion as oversized columns propagate before block verification rejects them. Gate 5: Spec cross-reference confirmed—01e property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-009 (outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:227-242) requires 'kzg_commitments count must not exceed max_blobs_per_block for the sidecar's epoch' with assertion 'forall sidecar: len(sidecar.kzg_commitments) <= get_blob_parameters(epoch).max_blobs_per_block'. Code clearly deviates from this requirement. Gate 6: P2P network vulnerabilities are in-scope per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json. Severity downgraded from HIGH to Medium due to client diversity constraint (Lighthouse ~35% network share caps single-client bug severity) and limited impact (resource exhaustion rather than consensus failure, as oversized blocks are still rejected during block verification).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: kzg_commitments count must not exceed max_blobs_per_block for the sidecar's epoch. (01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json, assertion: forall sidecar: len(sidecar.kzg_commitments) <= get_blob_parameters(epoch).max_blobs_per_block)
